name: Weddings
description: Wedding traditions from around the world — rings, dresses, rituals, and customs

questions.q: Which British queen popularized the white wedding dress, beginning with her own 1840 wedding?

source.notes: Her choice broke with the tradition of simply wearing one's best dress.

questions.q: On which finger of the left hand is a Western wedding ring traditionally worn?

source.notes: Counting from the thumb in the English system.

questions.q: Which Nevada city is nicknamed the 'Wedding Capital of the World' for its quickie marriages?

source.notes: Home to countless drive-thru wedding chapels.

questions.q: What item does a bride traditionally throw to her guests after the ceremony?

source.notes: The catcher is said to be next to marry.

questions.q: What tiny edible grain has traditionally been thrown at departing couples to symbolize fertility?

source.notes: Some venues now use dried flowers or confetti for bird-safety reasons.

questions.q: In a traditional Hindu wedding, what reddish-orange powder does the groom apply to the part of his bride's hair?

source.notes: Marks a woman as married in Hindu tradition.

questions.q: In a traditional Scottish wedding, which knee-length pleated garment does the groom typically wear?

source.notes: Often in the groom's family tartan.

questions.q: Which Roman-era concept — a mythical 'vein of love' running to the heart — historically justified the wedding ring's placement?

source.notes: Anatomically incorrect but culturally enduring.

questions.q: What is the Hindu pre-wedding ceremony in which intricate henna designs are applied to the bride?

source.notes: The darker the stain, the stronger the love, by tradition.

questions.q: In a Jewish wedding, what does the groom famously break with his foot at the end of the ceremony?

source.notes: Said to recall the destruction of the Temple in Jerusalem.

questions.q: What is the name of the Jewish seven-day celebration following a wedding?

source.notes: Literally 'seven blessings' — recited each evening.

questions.q: In a traditional Chinese wedding, the couple honors parents and elders through which ritual?

source.notes: Serves to formally introduce families to each other.

questions.q: What is the ancient Celtic/pagan ritual of literally binding the couple's hands together during a wedding?

source.notes: Origin of the phrase 'tying the knot.'

questions.q: Which term describes a wedding with fewer than about 20 guests, often a very intimate affair?

source.notes: Boomed in popularity during the COVID-19 pandemic.

questions.q: What type of wedding is held at a far-away resort or locale, with guests traveling to attend?

source.notes: Popular destinations include Italy, Mexico, and the Caribbean.

questions.q: What kind of wedding is historically one in which the marriage is arranged under pressure, often due to pregnancy?

source.notes: American idiom dating to at least the early 20th century.

questions.q: Which American reality show, set at Kleinfeld Bridal, revolves around brides shopping for wedding dresses?

source.notes: Long-running TLC hit since 2007.

questions.q: What is the name of the Hindu gold necklace a groom ties around his bride's neck during the wedding?

source.notes: Literally 'auspicious thread.'

questions.q: What is the name of the traditional Japanese white kimono worn by brides during the ceremony?

source.notes: Pure white to symbolize maidenhood and purity.

questions.q: What Vietnamese silk gown, meaning 'long shirt,' is traditionally worn by brides?

source.notes: Often red for weddings; worn over loose trousers.

questions.q: In a Bengali Hindu wedding, what tall conical headpiece does the groom traditionally wear?

source.notes: Made of cork-like pith; paired with the bride's red mukut.

questions.q: What is the Jewish tradition called where the couple declares each 'sanctified' to the other?

source.notes: The first part of the traditional Jewish wedding ceremony.
